facts.title: kv_cache_manager.allocate_slots takes Request, NOT request_id
facts.fact_full: Real signature: `allocate_slots(request, num_new_tokens, **kwargs)`. The first positional argument is the `Request` object itself, not its id. The kwargs are all prefix-cache or speculative-decode plumbing: `num_new_computed_tokens`, `new_computed_blocks`, `num_lookahead_tokens`, `num_external_computed_tokens`, `delay_cache_blocks`, `num_encoder_tokens`, `full_sequence_must_fit`. When simplifying, keep the first arg a Request so the call site reads identically to vLLM (`scheduler.py:L466-L471`).

facts.title: Phase 1 num_new_tokens == 0 uses continue, not break (deliberate FCFS relaxation)
facts.fact_full: scheduler.py:L446-L462 has an explicit comment: 'Here, by doing `continue` instead of `break`, we do not strictly follow the FCFS scheduling policy and allow the lower-priority requests to be scheduled.' This is intentional. Phase 2 still uses `break` for the chunked-prefill-disabled case (L684-L690) because there it's safer to wait than to skip ahead. Don't 'fix' Phase 1's `continue` to `break`.

facts.title: SchedulerOutput field is num_scheduled_tokens, not scheduled_requests
facts.discovered_by: implementer (during A1 rewrite, while reading legacy)
facts.fact_full: vLLM's authoritative field name is `num_scheduled_tokens: dict[str, int]` (output.py:L191-L193). The legacy implementation used `scheduled_requests`, which breaks 1:1 with the caller pattern at scheduler.py:L984-L987: `num_scheduled_tokens = scheduler_output.num_scheduled_tokens; for req_id, num in num_scheduled_tokens.items(): ...`. If a writer or tester copy-pastes that pattern verbatim from vLLM, `scheduled_requests` makes it AttributeError. Use `num_scheduled_tokens` for the dict, `total_num_scheduled_tokens` for the sum.

facts.title: SchedulerOutput.finished_req_ids cross-step carry — vLLM keeps it, our merged update_after_step clears it
facts.discovered_by: tester (Ch04 v5 test pass — fidelity finding F01)
facts.fact_full: vLLM's `SchedulerOutput.finished_req_ids` is populated *across* step boundaries: a request that finishes during step N's `update_from_output` is in `self.finished_req_ids` when step N+1's `schedule()` builds its output (scheduler.py:L919-L923). The model runner reads it on step N+1 to drop KV state. The vLLM source explicitly NOTEs at L995-L998: 'We shouldnt do `self.finished_req_ids.clear()` here because it will also affect the scheduler output.' In our Ch04 simplified `update_after_step`, we add freshly-finished IDs to `self.finished_req_ids` then clear at end of same call. Result: SchedulerOutput.finished_req_ids always empty. Authoritative finish check is `request.is_finished()`. For Ch20: clear at the START of `schedule()` to match vLLM 'between steps' semantics. Test: `test_finished_req_ids_field_is_simplified`.

facts.title: Preemption tests — sizing block pool for both-fit-then-OOM (W02 application)
facts.fact_full: Wisdom W02 (wisdom/testing.md): preemption tests must satisfy *both* requests can be admitted initially, *then* one needs more KV → triggers OOM → preempts the FCFS tail. Concrete sizing for `SimpleKVCacheManager(num_gpu_blocks, block_size=16)`: num_gpu_blocks=2, two prompts of 16 tokens each → both admit, 0 free blocks remain. Decode advances num_computed_tokens past the 16-token block boundary → next allocate_slots(req, 1) rounds up to 1 new block → OOM → preempted_req_ids non-empty. Pop-tail FCFS rule (K06): the LATER-added request gets preempted first. Drive with `_drive(sched, max_steps=80)` and assert the FIRST preempt step contains the tail-id.
